etymology.description: 𠤎 => ancient form of 化(化) (huà, "transform")
etymology.interpretation: 𠤎 is an ancient character depicting a person in an upside-down position. This form was part of the creation of the character 化(化) (huà, "transform"), representing the concept of reversal or change by using two 人(人) (rén, "person")—one upright and one upside down. This visual representation of inversion or change directly relates to the idea of transformation.
